fragility_points: Single-timestamp settlement risk is doing most of the bearish work.
A late weekend crypto downdraft could materially change the setup.
key_assumptions: Current roughly 5.8% cushion above $80 survives ordinary weekend volatility.
Binance SOL/USDT does not show a meaningful venue-specific dip at the resolving minute.
No major risk-off catalyst hits SOL before Sunday noon ET.
main_logical_chain: Verify the governing source and exact contract mechanic first.
Check current Binance SOL/USDT price and recent 1-minute candles directly.
Observe that SOL is currently about 5.8% above the threshold.
Net that cushion against timestamp-specific Sunday noon close risk.
Conclude Yes remains likely, but less certain than the 88.5% market implies.
main_thesis: SOL is already trading comfortably above $80, so Yes is more likely than not, but the single Binance noon-ET minute close keeps this below near-certainty.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified.
Direct Binance verification showed SOL/USDT around 84.67.
source_quality_view: Primary contract mechanics are strong and direct; current price verification is also direct via Binance API; independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium.
strongest_disconfirmers: This is a single-minute close market, so a badly timed dip at Sunday 12:00 ET can still resolve No.
Weekend altcoin volatility could erase a several-percent cushion quickly.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance verification showed SOLUSDT around 84.67-84.74, clearly above $80.
Polymarket rules confirm this is a Binance SOL/USDT close-above market, so current cushion is directly relevant.
No further upside is required if current regime broadly persists.
timing_relevance: The main catalyst is time passing without SOL losing the $80 buffer; the highest-information next check is a closer-to-resolution Binance verification pass.
unresolved_ambiguities: No final governing-source print exists yet because the relevant minute has not occurred.
Rules name the Binance interface candle display rather than an API endpoint.
what_would_change_view: I would turn more bearish if SOL fell toward or below 81 before Sunday or if a closer pass showed the cushion nearly gone; more bullish if late Saturday or Sunday checks still show a comfortable margin above $80.
